course_description:
This course expands introductory studies of soil mechanics with topics such as constitutive modeling, continuum mechanics, tensor calculus, elasticity, plasticity, problematic soils, unsaturated soils, soft soils, and the use of geophysics. This course is useful for structural and geotechnical engineers designing civil works. This course is beneficial for advanced computer simulations and analyses in geotechnics.

Generally, soil and geo-material comprise solid, water, and air phases. Consequently, this multi-phase mixture shows complex mechanical and hydraulic behaviour, which depends on water content, density, and degree of saturation. A deep understanding of soil and ground materials is required for the efficient and economical design of structures. This course extends students' introductory knowledge of soil mechanics and adds current subjects within geotechnical engineering. Advanced topics include contemporary new constitutive laws for material behaviour, problematic soils, such as unsaturated and soft soils,ﾠand the use of geophysics. This course is essential for structural and geotechnical engineers who analyse and design structures built with or in the ground. Finally, this course provides base knowledge for advanced geotechnical computer applications and data analyses.

course_aims: This course aims to present tensor calculus, continuum mechanics, material modelling, unsaturated soils, and geophysics for advanced soil mechanics and geotechnical engineering analyses.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply concepts for unsaturated soils to analyse mechanical, hydraulic, and environmental challenges.
Create stress-strain models for clays and sands.
Apply laboratory and field experiments to calibrate material models.
Evaluate assumptions for the representation of the mechanical behaviour of soils.
Analyse issues with soft and expansive soils.
Evaluate hydraulic principles of unsaturated soils.
Evaluate the impact of unsaturated soil mechanics in geotechnical engineering.
Analyse results from electrical resistivity tomography for soil characterisation.
Apply concepts of geophysics to provide solutions in geotechnical engineering.

assessment_details.title: Materials Modelling
assessment_details.task_description: Solution of a geotechnical problem involving, but not limited to, the following topics: (a) Constitutive relationships parameterisation and analysis, (b) Laboratory testing, and (c) Critical state soil mechanics. Only a few critical numerical results are required to be input via an online form. The problem definition and required results will be specified in a PDF file posted on Blackboard as the semester progresses. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The link to the online form will be specified in the PDF file posted on Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Final Exam
assessment_details.other_conditions: Time limited, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: The final exam will include short answer questions about materials modelling, unsaturated soils, and geophysics. This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Introduction Introduction to Advanced Soil Mechanics Learning outcomes: L01

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 5 (04 Aug - 31 Aug)
learning_activities.topic: Continuum mechanics and materials modelling Fundamentals of materials modelling and testing. Introduction to continuum mechanics, elasticity, and plasticity. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 12 (11 Aug - 26 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: Tutorials A set of tutorials will be defined in three parts covering: (1) materials modelling, (2) unsaturated soils, and (3) geophysics. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08, L09

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 9 (01 Sep - 28 Sep)
learning_activities.topic: Unsaturated soils Introduction to unsaturated, soft, and expansive soils. Physical principles, hydraulics, and case studies. Learning outcomes: L05, L06, L07

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 10 To Week 12 (06 Oct - 26 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: Geophysics Introduction to geophysics, electrical resistivity tomography for soil characterisation, induced polarization, and complex conductivity. Learning outcomes: L08, L09
Review Review and discussion about the final exam. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07, L08, L09
